neighborhoods.name: Harlem
neighborhoods.fact: 110th–155th St in central and east Manhattan. Starts where Central Park's northern edge ends. Does NOT span to the Hudson — the west side at this latitude is Hamilton Heights.

neighborhoods.name: Washington Heights
neighborhoods.fact: 155th–193rd St, sitting on Manhattan's highest ridge. Sandwiched between Harlem below and Inwood above. The island is noticeably narrow here.

neighborhoods.name: Inwood
neighborhoods.fact: Above 193rd St — the very northern tip of the island. Manhattan narrows to a point here with the Harlem River on the east and the Hudson on the west.

zones.name: Upper East & West Side
zones.street_range: 72nd to 110th Street · Central Park is the dividing line

neighborhoods.name: Upper East Side
neighborhoods.fact: East of Central Park, 72nd–96th St. Fifth Avenue is its western border — the park is literally across the street. Everything east of the park to the East River.

neighborhoods.name: Upper West Side
neighborhoods.fact: West of Central Park, 72nd–110th St. Squeezed between the park on the east and Riverside Park / the Hudson River on the west — two parks as its walls.

neighborhoods.name: Morningside Heights
neighborhoods.fact: Just above the northwest corner of Central Park, roughly 110th–125th St on the west side. Columbia University sits at its center on the ridge.

zones.name: Midtown
zones.street_range: 34th to 72nd Street · Penn Station & Grand Central are your anchors

neighborhoods.name: Midtown
neighborhoods.fact: The dense commercial core around 42nd–57th St. Times Square is dead center; Grand Central Terminal anchors 42nd & Park Ave on the east side.

neighborhoods.name: Hell's Kitchen
neighborhoods.fact: West side of Midtown, 34th–57th St, west of 8th Avenue toward the Hudson River piers. Everything between the theater district and the water.

neighborhoods.name: Murray Hill
neighborhoods.fact: East side of Midtown, roughly 30th–42nd St east of Park Ave. Just south of Grand Central, stretching toward the East River.

zones.name: Downtown
zones.street_range: 14th to 34th Street · The grid starts bending below 14th

neighborhoods.name: Chelsea
neighborhoods.fact: West side, 14th–30th St along the Hudson. The High Line elevated park runs through its middle north-south. Hudson Yards sits at its northern edge.

neighborhoods.name: Gramercy
neighborhoods.fact: East side, 14th–23rd St around Lexington Ave. Small and easy to miss — look east of Union Square, between Chelsea to the west and the East River.

neighborhoods.name: Greenwich Village
neighborhoods.fact: Below 14th St on the west side. The dead giveaway: the street grid goes diagonal and streets get names instead of numbers. West Village is its western slice.

zones.name: Lower Manhattan
zones.street_range: Below 14th Street · Streets have names, not numbers

neighborhoods.name: SoHo
neighborhoods.fact: South of Houston St, above Canal St, west of Lafayette. "South of Houston" — the name literally tells you where it is. Sits between Greenwich Village and Tribeca.

neighborhoods.name: Tribeca
neighborhoods.fact: Below Canal St, west of Broadway — a wedge shape pointing south. "Triangle Below Canal" is the name. Directly north of the Financial District.

neighborhoods.name: Financial District
neighborhoods.fact: The southern tip of the island, below Fulton St. Wall Street runs east-west near the bottom. One World Trade marks the northwest corner of the neighborhood.
